title: lepechinia radula
intro: Lepechinia radula, also known as yerba de la montana or mountain hyssop, is a plant species that belongs to the mint family and is native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lepechinia radula include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herb for treating colds, coughs, and flu. Lepechinia radula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and create a variety of herbal preparations.
